props.description: DBA-approved schema change on orders-pg. Flyway migration V812 drops idx_order_items_order_id, flagged 'unused' by pg_stat_user_indexes, to reclaim write throughput on the hot order_items table. No application read-path analysis attached.
props.short_description: Drop unused index idx_order_items_order_id (reclaim write IOPS)

props.description: HighLatencyP99 fired for orders-api (prod, tier-1) at 14:05 UTC. p99 jumped from ~90ms to 5.2s while p50 stayed flat at ~42ms — a tail-only regression, classic of a query that lost its index. Order submission is slow but not erroring. Change CHG-9 (a DBA index drop on orders.order_items) landed at 13:57, ~8m before onset.
props.short_description: orders-api p99 hit 5.2s ~8m after CHG-9 dropped an index
props.title: orders-api p99 latency spike
props.work_notes: HighLatencyP99 fired; p50 flat, tail blown. Suspect CHG-9.

props.author: dba-jsmith
props.message: V812: drop unused index idx_order_items_order_id on order_items

props.short_description: billing-api slow on order_items reads — same orders-pg
props.title: billing-api p99 latency spike

props.short_description: fulfillment-api slow queries on orders-pg order_items
props.title: fulfillment-api p99 latency spike

props.short_description: returns-api tail latency on the same shared orders-pg
props.title: returns-api elevated latency

props.statement: CHG-9 (DB migration) dropped an index on orders.order_items, forcing full-table scans that exhaust the JDBC connection pool and spike orders-api latency
A recent orders-api application code deploy introduced an inefficient/blocking code path

hypotheses.basis: post-fix: p99 back to ~95ms, JDBC exit-call latency and pool pressure gone, anomaly cleared — confirms the causal chain
hypotheses.statement: CHG-9 (DB migration) dropped an index on orders.order_items, forcing full-table scans that exhaust the JDBC connection pool and spike orders-api latency

hypotheses.basis: A recent orders-api application code deploy introduced an inefficient/blocking c
hypotheses.statement: A recent orders-api application code deploy introduced an inefficient/blocking code path

journal.narrative: orders-api p99 latency spiked to 5.2s at 14:05, 8 minutes after CHG-9 (a DB migration) landed at 13:57. ALT-1 (HighLatencyP99) fired. p50 stays flat at 46ms and errors at 1.5% — the tail alone is blown. Declared SEV2. Still bleeding; CMDB shows orders-api's only dependency is orders-pg. Investigate, don't blind-mitigate.

journal.goal: Normalize the signal: fix scope/severity/onset, seed the symptom (Anomaly) + subject + topology + candidate changes, and scope the impact.
journal.narrative: orders-api p99 latency spiked to 5.2s at 14:05, 8 minutes after CHG-9 (a DB migration) landed at 13:57. Change-first: CHG-9 (DB migration) landed 8m before onset — prime suspect (H1). ServiceNow surfaces 3 sibling apps (billing-api, fulfillment-api, returns-api) that filed the SAME latency symptom within the same 5-minute window — all share orders-pg. A co-firing cluster that tight is a hypothesis prior pointing at the shared DB/migration (H1), not any one app's code. No application deploy is on record; a code regression remains a weaker alternative (H2) pending investigation.

journal.goal: ONE loop — hypothesize ⇄ gather evidence ⇄ refute: generate causal explanations change-first, gather targeted evidence to confirm or refute the leader, rule out rivals.
journal.narrative: Change-first: CHG-9 (DB migration) landed 8m before onset — prime suspect (H1). ServiceNow surfaces 3 sibling apps (billing-api, fulfillment-api, returns-api) that filed the SAME latency symptom within the same 5-minute window — all share orders-pg. A co-firing cluster that tight is a hypothesis prior pointing at the shared DB/migration (H1), not any one app's code. No application deploy is on record; a code regression remains a weaker alternative (H2) pending investigation.

orders-api's own compute is fine (p50 flat at 42ms) — the tail is 100% JDBC-bound: get_snapshots pins the exit call to orders-pg, whose connection pool is maxed (200/200 via instant_query). git diff_range on CHG-9 shows the migration dropped an index, forcing full-table scans. H2 (code regression) refuted; H1 (migration/index) confirmed at high confidence.

Postmortem: CHG-9 (DB migration) dropped an index on orders.order_items -> full-table scans -> JDBC pool exhaustion -> orders-api latency spike; re-adding the index resolved it. An application code regression was investigated and ruled out.
